evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Batch to my .NET for Android app. Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes — every visible barcode should be tracked and added to a list as soon as it appears on screen.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit NuGet versions, writes complete BarcodeBatch integration code into EmptyActivity.cs (DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey, Camera.GetDefaultCamera, BarcodeBatch.RecommendedCameraSettings, BarcodeBatchSettings.Create with Symbology.Ean13Upca + Symbology.Code128, BarcodeBatch.Create, DataCaptureView.Create added to a FrameLayout container, BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.Create, IBarcodeBatchListener with OnSessionUpdated dispatching to RunOnUiThread, OnResume/OnPause/OnDestroy lifecycle), and shows the setup checklist (NuGet packages, AndroidManifest permission, runtime permission, MainApplication initialize for SDK 8.0+, layout container id, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding the Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages
Setup checklist mentions adding android.permission.CAMERA to AndroidManifest.xml
Setup checklist mentions requesting the CAMERA permission at runtime
Setup checklist mentions creating a MainApplication subclass that calls ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() and ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() (required for SDK 8.0+)
Setup checklist mentions the FrameLayout container in the activity layout (e.g. data_capture_view_container) that hosts the DataCaptureView
A Scandit license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
BarcodeBatchSettings.Create() is used (not new BarcodeBatchSettings(), which does not exist in the .NET binding)
BarcodeBatch.Create( is used (not new BarcodeBatch(...) and not BarcodeBatch.ForDataCaptureContext(...))
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
Camera.GetDefaultCamera() is used
BarcodeBatch.RecommendedCameraSettings is used as a static property (not BarcodeBatch.RecommendedCameraSettings() with parentheses and not createRecommendedCameraSettings())
camera.ApplySettingsAsync(...) is called with the recommended camera settings
dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync(camera) is called
DataCaptureView.Create( is used to create the preview view
The DataCaptureView is added to a FrameLayout container via container.AddView(...)
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.Create( is called and given both the BarcodeBatch and the DataCaptureView arguments
The activity implements IBarcodeBatchListener (or subscribes to barcodeBatch.SessionUpdated +=)
OnSessionUpdated is implemented with the signature (BarcodeBatch, BarcodeBatchSession, IFrameData) — not (BarcodeBatch, BarcodeBatchSession, FrameData)
Inside OnSessionUpdated, data from session.AddedTrackedBarcodes is copied out before scheduling UI work
UI work is dispatched via RunOnUiThread(() => ...)
OnResume sets barcodeBatch.Enabled = true and calls camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On)
OnPause sets barcodeBatch.Enabled = false and calls camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.Off)
barcodeBatch.RemoveListener(this) is called in OnDestroy
dataCaptureContext.RemoveCurrentMode() is called in OnDestroy
No BarcodeCapture.Create or IBarcodeCaptureListener appears (this is MatrixScan Batch, not single-shot BarcodeCapture)
No Kotlin-style symbology names (e.g. EAN13_UPCA, CODE128) appear
No SparkScan-style new SparkScan(...) constructor appears

evals.prompt: I want to highlight EAN-13 barcodes in green and CODE128 barcodes in blue in my MatrixScan Batch Activity. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements IBarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener, overrides BrushForTrackedBarcode to return colored Scandit Brushes per symbology, and assigns the listener to the overlay. The existing integration code (BarcodeBatch.Create, DataCaptureView.Create, BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.Create, BarcodeBatchListener, camera lifecycle) is preserved.

assertions.text: The class declares it implements IBarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener
BrushForTrackedBarcode(BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay overlay, TrackedBarcode trackedBarcode) is implemented
EAN-13 (Symbology.Ean13Upca) returns a green Scandit Brush
CODE128 (Symbology.Code128) returns a blue Scandit Brush
The overlay's Listener property is assigned to this (or to an instance of the listener)
The Scandit Brush type is used (Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Style.Brush), not Android.Graphics.Paint or a raw Color
Existing BarcodeBatch.Create call is preserved (not rewritten as new BarcodeBatch)
Existing IBarcodeBatchListener implementation (OnSessionUpdated, OnObservationStarted, OnObservationStopped) is preserved
Existing camera lifecycle in OnResume / OnPause is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Activity I want to log all currently tracked barcodes — their tracking ID and data — on every frame. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill updates OnSessionUpdated to iterate over session.TrackedBarcodes and log each TrackedBarcode's Identifier and Barcode.Data. Data must be copied out of the session before RunOnUiThread, because the session is only safe to access on the recognition thread.

assertions.text: session.TrackedBarcodes (the IDictionary<int, TrackedBarcode>) is accessed inside OnSessionUpdated
Each TrackedBarcode's Identifier is read
Each TrackedBarcode's Barcode.Data is read
The identifier+data pairs are copied into a local collection before RunOnUiThread is called (session.TrackedBarcodes is not accessed inside the UI dispatch lambda)
A logging call (Android.Util.Log, System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine, Console.WriteLine, or similar) is used to emit each tracked barcode
Existing BarcodeBatch.Create / Camera / DataCaptureView / BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay setup is preserved
Existing OnResume / OnPause / OnDestroy lifecycle is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Activity I'd rather have the highlight on each tracked barcode shown as a dot instead of the default rectangular frame. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill changes the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.Create call to pass BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot as the style argument (replacing BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Frame). It explains that the style is fixed at creation time (the Style property is read-only) and that Frame is the default. All other integration code (BarcodeBatch.Create, Camera, DataCaptureView, IBarcodeBatchListener, lifecycle) is preserved.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.Create is called with BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot as the style argument
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Frame is no longer passed to BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.Create (it is replaced by .Dot, since the answer asked for the dot style)
The style enum value is named BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot (semantic) — not a string, not a 'dotRadius' setting, and not a Kotlin/Swift-style .dot lowercase token
The using Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Batch.UI.Overlay; directive is present for the overlay and style types
The answer notes the overlay style is chosen at Create time / the Style property is read-only (the style cannot be changed on an existing overlay) (semantic)
Existing BarcodeBatch.Create call is preserved (not rewritten as new BarcodeBatch)
Existing IBarcodeBatchListener implementation (OnSessionUpdated, OnObservationStarted, OnObservationStopped) is preserved
Existing OnResume / OnPause / OnDestroy camera lifecycle is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Activity I want to anchor a small custom label (a TextView showing the barcode's data) on top of each tracked barcode, and have it move with the barcode. I have the MatrixScan AR add-on. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay: creates it with BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay.Create(barcodeBatch, dataCaptureView), makes the Activity implement IBarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayListener, assigns the overlay's Listener, and implements ViewForTrackedBarcode to return an Android TextView whose text is the tracked barcode's data. It notes the advanced overlay requires the MatrixScan AR add-on and that ViewForTrackedBarcode is called on the main thread. The existing BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay and integration code are preserved.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay.Create( is called with the BarcodeBatch and the DataCaptureView (not new BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay(...))
The Activity declares it implements IBarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayListener
The advanced overlay's Listener property is assigned (e.g. advancedOverlay.Listener = this)
ViewForTrackedBarcode(BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay overlay, TrackedBarcode trackedBarcode) is implemented and returns an Android View (e.g. a TextView)
The returned view's text is set from trackedBarcode.Barcode.Data
The using Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Batch.UI.Overlay; directive is present
The answer notes the advanced overlay requires the MatrixScan AR add-on (semantic)
Existing BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.Create call is preserved (the advanced overlay is added in addition, not as a replacement) (semantic)
Existing IBarcodeBatchListener implementation and OnResume / OnPause / OnDestroy lifecycle are preserved

evals.prompt: I'm already anchoring a label view to each tracked barcode with BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay in my MatrixScan Batch Activity. Now I want the label to sit centered just above each barcode rather than overlapping it. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements AnchorForTrackedBarcode to return Anchor.TopCenter and OffsetForTrackedBarcode to return a PointWithUnit built from FloatWithUnit values using MeasureUnit.Fraction, with a negative vertical fraction to push the label above the barcode. It explains that the offset is relative to the anchor and that a fractional unit scales with the view size. The existing ViewForTrackedBarcode and integration code are preserved.

assertions.text: AnchorForTrackedBarcode(BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay overlay, TrackedBarcode trackedBarcode) is implemented and returns Anchor.TopCenter
OffsetForTrackedBarcode(BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay overlay, TrackedBarcode trackedBarcode) is implemented and returns a PointWithUnit
The PointWithUnit is constructed from two FloatWithUnit values (x and y)
Each FloatWithUnit uses MeasureUnit.Fraction as its unit
The vertical (y) FloatWithUnit uses a negative value to position the label above the barcode
The anchor value is Anchor.TopCenter (semantic) — not a string and not a lowercase Kotlin/Swift-style token
The using Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Common.Geometry; directive is present for Anchor, PointWithUnit, FloatWithUnit and MeasureUnit
Existing ViewForTrackedBarcode / BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay setup is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Activity I want the device to beep and vibrate whenever a new barcode starts being tracked. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that BarcodeBatch (unlike SparkScan or Barcode Capture) has NO automatic feedback, so feedback must be emitted manually. It creates a Feedback instance (Feedback.DefaultFeedback, or new Feedback(Vibration.DefaultVibration, Sound.DefaultSound)) held as a field, and in OnSessionUpdated, when session.AddedTrackedBarcodes is non-empty, calls feedback.Emit(). It uses the Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Common.Feedback namespace. The existing integration and lifecycle code are preserved.

assertions.text: A Scandit Feedback instance is created via Feedback.DefaultFeedback (a static property, not Feedback.DefaultFeedback() with parentheses) or via the new Feedback(Vibration, Sound) constructor
feedback.Emit() is called to play the feedback
Emit() is called inside OnSessionUpdated, gated on session.AddedTrackedBarcodes being non-empty (so it fires only for newly-tracked barcodes)
The using Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Common.Feedback; directive is present (the namespace for Feedback, Vibration and Sound)
The answer states that BarcodeBatch has no automatic feedback and that feedback must be emitted manually (semantic)
No BarcodeBatchSettings feedback / Feedback property is assumed on the mode or settings (BarcodeBatch exposes no feedback setting — it is emitted manually) (semantic)
Existing BarcodeBatch.Create / Camera / DataCaptureView / BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay setup is preserved
Existing OnResume / OnPause / OnDestroy lifecycle is preserved
